data.title: Hawkins buys StillWaters Technology
data.description:
• Hawkins (NASDAQ:HWKN) has acquired StillWaters Technology to further expand its water treatment business and build out its southern U.S. presence.
  • StillWaters distributes water treatment chemicals and equipment for its customers in Alabama.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Canada GDP drops 0.4% in Q2
data.description:
• The GDP in Canada contracted 0.40% in Q2 2025 over the previous quarter, vs. previous month's growth of 0.5%

  • M/M GDP figure dropped 0.1%

  • ETFs: (NYSEARCA:EWC), (NYSEARCA:FLCA).

  • Currency: (USD:CAD) (CAD:USD)

data.title: Dell's AI momentum continues to impress Wall Street amid spending boom
data.description:
Amid the spending boom on all things related to artificial intelligence, Dell Technologies (NYSE:DELL) has proven itself time and time again to be a beneficiary. And judging by its latest quarterly results and guidance, its position is getting stronger.

Shares fell 6% in premarket trading on Friday, while competitor Super Micro Computer (SMCI) also lost ground.

The IT said it now sees $20B in AI server revenue in fiscal 2026, up from a prior outlook of more than $15B, amid “exceptional” demand for its AI solutions, according to Dell COO Jeff Clarke. As such, Mizuho Securities analyst Vijay Rakesh reiterated his Outperform rating and upped his price target to $160. 

“We see DELL well-positioned with AI server share gains, strong AI PC refresh cycle tailwinds into 2025E,” Rakesh wrote in a note to clients, adding that there should be an increase in second-half margins as Nvidia's (NVDA) Blackwell line of GPUs ramps up.

Wells Fargo analyst Aaron Rakers noted that the stock traded lower after the results amid concerns about the aforementioned margins, but said he would be a buyer of the stock on a pullback. Rakers also upped his price target to $160 from $150 and reiterated his Overweight rating.

Looking ahead, Rakers said the company's analyst day, set for October 7, could be a positive for the stock.

For the fiscal third-quarter, Dell anticipates adjusted earnings of $2.45 per share at the midpoint, while sales are forecast to be between $26.5B and $27.5B. Analysts were anticipating earnings of $2.55 per share on $26.4B in revenue.

For the full-year, it expects to earn $9.55 per share, excluding one-time items, at the midpoint, above the $9.40 per share estimate. Full-year revenue is expected to be between $105B and $109B, with the $107B midpoint above the $105.03B estimate.

data.title: Wholesale inventories rise 0.2% M/M in July, as expected; retail inventories +0.2%
data.description:
Advance wholesale inventories: +0.2% M/M to $908.4B vs. +0.2% consensus and +0.1% prior, according to data released by the U.S. Census Bureau on Friday. 

Advance retail inventories: +0.2% vs. +0.2% prior.

Excluding autos, retail inventories: +0.1% vs. -0.1% prior.

data.title: Lululemon CEO sells $160M of Amer Sports stock
data.description:
Shares of Amer Sports (NYSE:AS) are under pressure in early Friday trading after a regulatory filing disclosed a large block of shares were sold by Lululemon (LULU) founder Chip Wilson.

Wilson sold ~$4M shares in the sports equipment maker for a total value of just under $160M. Following the sale, he retains an 18% stake in Amer Sports (NYSE:AS).

While the sale was reportedly unrelated to Wilson’s confidence in Amer Sports’ (NYSE:AS) performance and instead appears to be an effort to raise cash, the news weighed on shares of Amer Sports (AS), driving the stock down nearly 2% in premarket trading.

According to Bloomberg, Wilson’s transaction occurs just weeks after pledging Lululemon (LULU) shares in return for cash for the third time in just over a year.

Wilson was part of a group of investors led by China’s Anta Sports that acquired the company in 2019, ultimately taking the Helsinki-based company public in February 2024.
